Thala reveals it had suffered a “security breach” due to an “isolated vulnerability” related to its v1 farming contracts, which allowed the attacker to withdraw liquidity tokens. The company is able to recover $25.5 million of liquidity pool tokens
Proofpoint researchers identify a campaign using GitHub notifications to deliver malware.

Researchers at Proofpoint identify a NetSupport campaign that used “benign” email messages to instruct users to copy and paste PowerShell into their terminal that ultimately delivered the NetSupport RAT.
Researchers at Proofpoint identify a campaign delivering Brute Ratel C4 and Latrodectus.

author: UAC-0050
description: Researchers at Proofpoint identify a Ukrainian language campaign, targeting organizations in Ukraine, purporting to be emails sharing documents or requested information with the recipient.
Exploitation attempts are seen for CVE-2024-8068 and CVE-2024-8069, two recently patched Citrix Session Recording vulnerabilities.
Researchers at Bitdefender uncover a malicious advertising (malvertising) campaign that exploits Meta’s advertising platform to distribute malware on Facebook.

International Game Technology (IGT), one of the largest gambling companies in the U.S. says that a cyberattack caused massive disruptions to their operations, forcing them to take some systems offline.

author: abyss0

Amazon, Amazon Music, and Audible are flooded with bogus listings that push dubious "forex trading" sites, Telegram channels, and suspicious links claiming to offer pirated software.

author: nears' (previously near2tlg)

The company confirms that hackers have compromised a MediBoard account. However, it noted that this was not the result of a software vulnerability or misconfiguration on their part, but rather through the use of stolen credentials used by the hospital.

The Pacific Pulmonary Medical Group (PPMG) is hit with an Everest Team ransomware attack.

author: State-sponsored Threat Actors
target: Multiple Romanian election sites, including bec.ro (Central Election Bureau), roaep.ro, and registrulelectoral.ro (voter registration)
description: A report from Romania’s Intelligence Service (SRI) says that the country’s election infrastructure was targeted by more than 85,000 cyberattacks with threat actors also obtaining access credentials for election-related websites and leaked them on a Russian forum less than a week before the first presidential election round.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

author: Roaming Mantis
description: Researchers at Hunt.io discover a MoqHao (a.k.a. Wroba and XLoader) targeting Apple IDs and Android devices in Japan.

author: Ukraine’s military intelligence agency (HUR)
description: Russian users report difficulties accessing services at Gazprombank, one of the country’s largest privately owned banks, following an alleged cyberattack by Ukraine’s military intelligence agency.

The Japanese corporation Ito En confirms that its U.S. subsidiary was hit with ransomware. The company is the largest producer of green tea in Japan and has subsidiaries in the U.S., Australia, China and Indonesia.

target: Ukrainian Defense Companies and Security and Defense Forces
description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) discover a series of phishing emails targeting Ukrainian defense companies and security and defense forces with a fake NATO standards conference.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: The U.S. subsidiary of Kurita Water, a Japanese water treatment company says ransomware actors have stolen data from systems and encrypted some servers.
Privileged access management company BeyondTrust suffers a cyberattack after threat actors breached some of its Remote Support SaaS instances.

Researchers at ReversingLabs discover a malicious version of the popular AI library ultralytics — which has almost 60 million downloads — published to the Python Package Index (PyPI) package repository, and containing downloader code that was downloading the XMRig coinminer. The compromise of the project's build environment was achieved by exploiting a known and previously reported GitHub Actions script injection.
​Electrica Group, a key player in the Romanian electricity distribution and supply market, is investigating a ransomware attack.

author: Lynx
description: ​Artivion, a leading manufacturer of heart surgery medical devices, discloses q ransomware attack that disrupted its operations and forced it to take some systems offline.

Researchers at Socket discover a malicious npm package targeting developers attempting to install the popular @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin package, an ESLint plugin specifically for TypeScript code.

author: Smishing Triad
description: Researchers at Resecurity identify a wide-scale fraudulent campaign targeting consumers in the UAE by impersonating the Dubai law enforcement.

Healthcare software as a service (SaaS) company Phreesia notifies over 910,000 people that their personal and health data was exposed in a May breach of its subsidiary ConnectOnCall, acquired in October 2023.
US Bitcoin ATM operator Byte Federal discloses a data breach that exposed the data of 58,000 customers after its systems were breached using a GitLab vulnerability.
More than 240,000 people have information stolen during a cyberattack on SRP Federal Credit Union, one of the largest in South Carolina.
Kitsap Mental Health Services discloses to have suffered a cyberattack due to an unauthorized access to their network.

author: Rhysida
description: Granite School District discloses that a cyber attack affected all the students' records. The Rhysida ransomware gang claims responsibility for the attack.

Threat actors claim they stole 17 million patient records from PIH Health, a southern California regional healthcare provider that is still struggling with IT and phone systems outages that have been disrupting patient care since the organization was hit by a ransomware attack on Dec. 1.

Boston University’s renowned Framingham Heart Study (FHS) is breached by threat actors, who gained access to the data of participants—both living and deceased—of the country’s longest running, multigenerational heart study.

author: Interlock
target: Texas Tech University Health Sciences Center
description: The Texas Tech University Health Sciences Center and its El Paso counterpart suffer a cyberattack that disrupted computer systems and applications, potentially exposing the data of 1.4 million patients.

author: Holy League
description: The hacktivist collective Holy League launches a DDoS campaign against French governmental and critical infrastructure.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

author: Holy League
description: The hacktivist collective Holy League launches a DDoS campaign against undisclosed targets in Germany.

author: TA397 a.k.a. Bitter
description: Researchers at Proofpoint discover a sophisticated phishing attack targeting a Turkish defense sector organization via the MiyaRAT, carried out by the threat actor TA397, a.k.a. Bitter, using a Madagascar lure, and delivering malware through advanced mechanisms involving NTFS Alternate Data Streams (ADS) and scheduled tasks.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: The Gem Pad token launchpad is exploited for an estimated loss of $2.2M in crypto assets.

author: UAC-0125 linked to UAC-0002, a.k.a. APT44, FROZENBARENTS, Sandworm, Seashell Blizzard, and Voodoo Bear
description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) discloses that a threat actor tracked as UAC-0125 is leveraging Cloudflare Workers to trick military personnel in the country into downloading malware disguised as Army+, a mobile app that was introduced by the Ministry of Defence back in August 2024 in an effort to make the armed forces go paperless.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Adam Griffin, a crypto investor is robbed of nearly $500,000 in cryptocurrencies after a scammer called using a real Google phone number to warn his Gmail account was being hacked, sent email security alerts directly from google.com, and ultimately seized control over the account by convincing him to click “yes” to a Google prompt on his mobile device.

author: XakNet
description: Suspected Russian threat actors from the XakNet collective launch one of the largest cyberattacks on Ukraine’s state services in recent months.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Researchers at Sonatype reveal that the supply chain attack targeting Rspack is also said to have targeted another npm package named vant.

author: “solidit-dev-416”
description: Researchers at Socket discover a malicious package on the npm package registry that masquerades as a library for detecting vulnerabilities in Ethereum smart contracts but, in reality, drops the open-source remote access trojan called Quasar RAT onto developer systems.
The Illinois Department Of Human Services reveals that a phishing attack affected more than 1.1M public assistance clients.
Personal data from current and former students in Boone County School District are compromised in a phishing scheme.
Personal data from current and former students in Kenton County School District are compromised in a phishing scheme.
The European Space Agency's official web shop is hacked as it started to load a piece of JavaScript code that generates a fake Stripe payment page at checkout.
Pittsburgh Regional Transit (PRT) is hit with a ransomware attack.
A ransomware attack on American Addiction Centers exposes the sensitive healthcare information of more than 400,000 people. The Rhysida ransomware gang claims responsibility for the attack.
Aerospace and defense giant General Dynamics says threat actors compromised dozens of employee benefits accounts after a successful phishing campaign targeting its personnel.

The Richmond University Medical Center in New York is investigating a ransomware attack since May 2023 and it recently determined that the incident resulted in a data breach affecting more than 670,000 people.

ZAGG informs customers that their credit card data has been exposed to unauthorized individuals after hackers compromised a third-party application provided by the company's e-commerce provider, BigCommerce.

author: “h4xx0r1337″
description: The Mi Argentina site and the SUBE card app, two of the government’s most important digital platforms, suffer a cyber attack.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

author: “h4xx0r1337″
description: The Mi Argentina site and the SUBE card app, two of the government’s most important digital platforms, suffer a cyber attack.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Japan’s flag carrier announces that it has restored its systems following a cyber incident that delayed some domestic and international flights.
Data-loss prevention startup Cyberhaven says threat actors published a malicious update to its Chrome extension that was capable of stealing customer passwords and session tokens.

target: X account of Animoca Brands co-founder Yat Siu
description: A threat actor compromises the X account of Animoca Brands co-founder Yat Siu, using it to promote a fraudulent token.
A new attack campaign targets known Chrome browser extensions, leading to at least 35 extensions being compromised and exposing over 2.6 million users to data exposure and credential theft.
Rutherford County Schools confirm to be dealing with a “network and systems disruption” since November 25.

Infocert, one of the leading Spid digital identity providers in Italy, confirms a serious breach of its users’ personal data. The attackers claim to have stolen a huge volume of data from Infocert’s databases, including 5.5 million registrations, 1.1 million telephone numbers, and 2.5 million email addresses.

author: Noname057(16)
target: Multiple Italian sites, including Malpensa and Linate airports
description: Pro-Russia group Noname057(16) targets Italian sites, including Malpensa and Linate airports, in a new DDoS campaign amid rising geopolitical tensions.

author: 0mid16B
description: The threat actor known as 0mid16B breaches DEphoto, a U.K. photo business, twice in few days, acquiring the personal information of 555,952 customers.
Ford confirms that its X account was briefly compromised, after posts referencing the Israel-Palestine war are published.
Japan’s largest mobile carrier, NTT Docomo, reports that it is working to restore services after a cyberattack temporarily disrupted operations.

Bank of America notifies 414 individuals that their names, addresses, phone numbers, passport numbers, Social Security numbers, and their mortgage load numbers might have been compromised in a data breach at an unnamed third-party provider.

Osaka-based Resona Bank says a DDoS attack resulted in a network malfunction and impacted the functioning of its customer-facing My Gate application. The parent company Resona Holdings confirms the incident temporarily disrupted services at other company-owned banks, including Minato Bank, Kansai Mirai Bank and Saitama Resona Bank.
Mizuho Bank, Japan's third largest financial company, suffers a denial-of-service attack that disrupts online banking services for three hours.

The Green Bay Packers American football team notifies fans that a threat actor hacked its official online retail store in October and injected a card skimmer script to steal customers' personal and payment information.
South Portland Public Schools in Maine says it was forced to take its network after a cyberattack was discovered over the weekend.

author: Ukrainian Cyber Alliance
description: ​Ukrainian hacktivists, part of the Ukrainian Cyber Alliance group, announce they had breached Russian internet service provider Nodex's network and wiped hacked systems after stealing sensitive documents.

​Medusind, a leading billing provider for healthcare organizations, notifies hundreds of thousands of individuals of a data breach that exposed their personal and health information more than a year ago, in December 2023. 360,934 individuals are affected.

author: Everest
description: Popular cannabis brand STIIIZY discloses a data breach after threat actors breached its point-of-sale (POS) vendor to steal customer information, including government IDs and purchase information. The Everest. ransomware group claims responsibility for the attack.

author: Monti
description: Excelsior Orthopaedics notifies approximately 357,000 people that their personal and health information was compromised in a data breach resulting from a ransomware attack that came to light in June 2024.
The Addison Northwest School District (ANWSD) suffers a ransomware attack.
Gateshead Council suffers a cyber attack.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Orange Finances announces that a threat actor compromised the admin address, upgraded contracts, and transferred funds to their wallet for an estimated loss of more than $840K.

author: RansomHub
description: ​BayMark Health Services, North America's largest provider of substance use disorder (SUD) treatment and recovery services, notifies an undisclosed number of patients that attackers stole their personal and health information in a September 2024 breach. The RansomHub operation claimed responsibility for the breach.
Winston-Salem, North Carolina, residents are not able to pay their utility bills online after a post-Christmas cyberattack knocked the city’s systems offline.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Moby Trade is the victim of a $2.5 million hack exploiting a compromised private key. However, a mistake by the attacker allows a whitehat to steal $1.5 million back and return it to the protocol.

Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP ("Wolf Haldenstein") a U.S. Law Firm, reports it has suffered a data breach that exposed the personal information of nearly 3.5 million individuals to threat actors.

author: DNA, Grep, Pryx, and Rey (Hellcat ransomware group)
description: Spanish telecommunications company Telefónica confirms an internal ticketing system was breached after stolen data is leaked on a hacking forum.

target: Slovakian Geodesy, Cartography and Cadastre Office (UGKK)
description: A cyber attack targets the Slovakian Geodesy, Cartography and Cadastre Office (UGKK), which manages land and property data. The agency’s systems are shut down, and its physical offices closed following an alleged ransomware attack. According to local media reports, the attackers are demanding millions of euros in ransom.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: The government of Turks and Caicos says is making progress in its recovery from a recent ransomware attack that has caused widespread issues on the islands.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Researchers at Wiz reveal that threat actors are exploiting a critical remote command execution vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2024-50603, in Aviatrix Controller instances to install backdoors and crypto miners.

author: Qilin
description: The government of West Haven, Connecticut, says it is investigating a cyberattack that recently forced it to temporarily shut down all of its IT systems. The Qilin ransomware group claims responsibilty for the attack.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

author: NoName057(16)
description: Pro-Russia threat actors from Noname057(16) target Italian ministries, institutions, critical infrastructure’s websites and private organizations in coincidence with the visit of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky to Italy.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Eindhoven University of Technology cancels “lectures and other educational activities” following a cyberattack, although it is expected to have only a limited impact as teaching is reduced while students prepare for exams.

author: NoName057(16)
description: Pro-Russia threat actors from Noname057(16) target again Italian ministries, institutions, critical infrastructure’s websites and private organizations in coincidence with the visit of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky to Italy.

author: DragonForce
description: Teton Orthopaedics discloses a DragonForce ransomware attack. A total of 13,409 people are affected by the incident.

author: Yellow Drift
description: Roseltorg, Russia’s main electronic trading platform for government and corporate procurement confirms that it had been targeted by a cyberattack. Pro-Ukraine hacker group Yellow Drift claims responsibility.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

author: UAC-0063, overlapping with APT28, a.k.a. Blue Athena, BlueDelta, Fancy Bear, Fighting Ursa, Forest Blizzard, FROZENLAKE, Iron Twilight, ITG05, Pawn Storm, Sednit, Sofacy, and TA422.
description: Researchers at Sekoia attribute the Russia-linked threat actors from APT28 to an ongoing cyber espionage campaign targeting Kazakhstan as part of the Kremlin's efforts to gather economic and political intelligence in Central Asia.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Avery Products Corporation warns it suffered a data breach after its website was hacked to steal customers' credit cards and personal information.
A Corvallis city councilor’s email is hacked by a scammer and used to reach nearly 3,500 email addresses in an attempt to steal the recipients' personal information.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: An attacker accesses Kong’s DockerHub account and replaces the legitimate Kong Ingress Controller v.3.4.0 image with a malicious version containing malicious code that enabled cryptojacking.

author: Black Basta
description: Mortgage Investors Group (MIG), one of the largest mortgage lenders in the Southeast U.S. says it suffered a cybersecurity incident last month that exposed troves of customer information. The Black Basta ransomware group claims responsibility for the attack.

author: Fog
description: The University of Oklahoma says it is taking steps to address unusual cyber activity it discovered on its network. The Fog ransomware group claims responsibility for the attack.

author: “0mid16B”
description: A threat actor with the Moniker “0mid16B” claims to have breached MedSave Health Insurance TPA Ltd (“MedSave”), stealing 561 gigabytes of databases, containing data of 10,617,943 people.
The Idols NFT contract on Ethereum suffers an exploit resulting in a loss of approximately $340K.
The Town of Ulster discloses a ransomware attack.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

author: INC Ransom
description: The International AIDS Vaccine Initiative (IAVI) discloses a ransomware attack. The INC Ransom group claims responsibility.
Allegheny Health Network discloses that an "unauthorized user" hacked its IT vendor IntraSystems.

Threat actors abuse Google ads to spread malware, using a fake Homebrew website to infect Macs and Linux devices with AmosStealer (aka 'Atomic'), an infostealer that steals credentials, browser data, and cryptocurrency wallets.
Blacon High School announces a temporary closure after falling victim to a "ransomware attack".

target: Unnamed internet service provider (ISP) from Eastern Asia
description: Cloudflare says it detected and blocked a 5.6 Terabit per second (Tbps) distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack, the largest ever attack to be reported to date.
Researchers at Hunt.io discover a campaign targeting software developers through malicious Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extensions.
Threat actors on X are exploiting the news around Ross Ulbricht to direct unsuspecting users to a Telegram channel that tricks them into run PowerShell code that infects them with malware.
American business services and government contractor Conduent confirms that a recent outage resulted from what it described as a "cyber security incident."

author: Threat Actors from North Korea?
description: Singapore-based cryptocurrency platform Phemex is forced to pause some of its operations after a suspected cyberattack led to the theft of more than $85 million in digital coins.
Matagorda County discloses a cyber attack “involving a virus that has affected several internal systems.”
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

author: Handala
description: The pro-Palestinian group called Handala reportedly breaches emergency systems used in Israeli schools, after compromising Maagar-Tec, and broadcasts rocket sirens and Arabic songs that Israel’s cyber agency called supportive of “terrorism.”
Marposs is hit with a ransomware attack
Chinese AI platform DeepSeek disables registrations on its DeepSeek-V3 chat platform due to an ongoing "large-scale" cyberattack targeting its services.
Frederick Health Medical Group warns that there will be delays in service as it is hit by a ransomware attack.
A cyberattack forces the government-run South African Weather Service (SAWS) offline, limiting access to a critical service used by the country’s airlines, farmers and allies.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Law firm Berman & Rabin notifies roughly 152,000 individuals that their personal information was compromised in a July 2024 ransomware attack.
​The New York Blood Center (NYBC), one of the world's largest independent blood collection and distribution organizations, says a Sunday ransomware attack forced it to reschedule some appointments.
NorthBay Healthcare Corporation in California notifies 569,012 people of a breach that occurred between January 11 to April 1, 2024.
Community Health Center (CHC), a leading Connecticut healthcare provider, notifies over 1 million patients of a data breach that impacted their personal and health data.

author: BianLian
description: ​Mizuno USA, a subsidiary of Mizuno Corporation, one of the world's largest sporting goods manufacturers, confirms in data breach notification letters that unknown attackers stole files from its network between August and October 2024. The BianLian claims responsibility for the attack.

author: Akira
description: Yazoo Valley Electric Power Association, an electric utility serving multiple counties in Mississippi discloses to suffer an attack by cybercriminals last summer in an incident that exposed the information of more than 20,000 residents. The Akira ransomware gang claims responsibility for the attack.

author: 0mid16B
description: Apex Custom Software is hit with a ransomware attack.
Non-profit hospital district Delta County Memorial Hospital informs that threat actors had compromised the personal information of 148,363 people in a May 2024 cyberattack.

author: DragonForce
description: Heart Centre in Australia is hit with a DragonForce ransomware attack.
A DDoS attack targets Bohemia Interactive's infrastructure, preventing players of DayZ and Arma Reforger from playing the games online.
McKinney, a large suburb of Dallas, informs thousands of residents that a cyberattack in October exposed sensitive information.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Ionic Money confirms a serious security breach, with estimated losses exceeding $8.6 million.

target: Seven Italians and Individuals in Belgium, Greece, Latvia, Lithuania, Austria, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Germany, the Netherlands, Portugal, Spain and Sweden
description: The Italian government reveals that seven Italians and victims in more than a dozen other European countries were targeted with spyware as part of a broad hacking campaign previously revealed by WhatsApp.
country: AT CY CZ BE DE DK ES GR IT LT LV NL PT SE

description: Avery, the world’s largest supplier of labels says a ransomware attack in December prompted an investigation that led to the discovery of a data breach impacting the information of about 67,000 customers.
Lee Enterprises, one of the largest newspaper groups in the United States, says a cyberattack that hit its systems caused an outage and impacted its operations.
A ransomware gang shuts down the internet and telephone systems used by the University of The Bahamas, forcing changes on administrators, professors and students.

University Diagnostic Medical Imaging (UDMI), a medical imaging center in New York, says threat actors accessed certain information on its systems for a brief period on November 26, before the suspicious activity was discovered. 138,080 individuals are impacted by the data breach.

author: Embargo
description: Memorial Hospital and Manor informs approximately 120,000 individuals that their sensitive information was compromised in a ransomware attack that occurred in November 2024.
Humboldt Independent Practice Association discloses a phishing incident with an unauthorized actor gaining access to a single email account.

author: RansomHub
target: Sault Ste. Marie Tribe of Chippewa Indians
description: A ransomware attack on the Sault Tribe in Michigan knocks many of its most critical services offline. The threat actor claims to have stolen have acquired 119 GB of data (501,211 files).
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Decentralized money lender zkLend suffers a breach where threat actors exploited a smart contract flaw to steal 3,600 Ethereum, worth $9.5 million at the time.

author: Sarcoma
description: A relatively new ransomware operation named ‘Sarcoma’ claims responsibility for an attack against the Unimicron printed circuit boards (PCB) maker in Taiwan.

author: NoName057(16)
target: Several Italian organizations, including Intesa San Paolo bank, Milan's Linate and Malpensa airports, the Transport Authority, and ports of Taranto and Trieste.
description: Pro-Russia hacker group, NoName057(16), launches DDoS attacks against Italian banks and airports. The attacks are a response to Italy's support for Ukraine, as stated by the group on their Telegram channel.

Ecuador's National Assembly reports two cyberattacks aimed at disrupting its systems and accessing sensitive data. The attacks happened a week after the country's general election. The assembly counteracted the situation but provided no further details. The country's national civil registry and election agency have also been recently targeted by similar attacks.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Venture capital giant Insight Partners is hit by a sophisticated social engineering attack. The attack gained unauthorized access to their information systems, potentially compromising banking, tax, and personal data.

A security breach affects thousands of wallets connected to Cardex, a blockchain-based game. The attack, identified as a "session key hack," allows a malicious actor to exploit a compromised session signer wallet shared by all Cardex users. A leaked key in Cardex's frontend code enabled the attacker to conduct unauthorized transactions, draining $400,000 from 9,000 wallets.

author: Termite
description: An Australian fertility services provider, Genea, is hit by a security breach where an unauthorized third party accessed Genea's network and data.
A business email compromise (BEC) scheme against mining company NioCorp results in a loss of $500,000 in misdirected vendor payments, after threat actors breached its information and email systems.

author: Cyber Alliance
description: A pro-Ukraine hacking group, Cyber Alliance, claims responsibility for a cyberattack on Russian microfinance company CarMoney, which destroyed its infrastructure and allegedly compromised customer data.

Fashion Box S.p.A., the Italian parent company of Replay Jeans, is hit by a cyberattack. A threat actor used a brute force attack to access the company's servers, exfiltrating confidential and personal data, including stakeholder information.

author: RansomHouse
description: The RansomHouse group claims responsibility for stealing data from the Supreme Administrative Court’s information systems.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

author: Lazarus Group
description: Cryptocurrency exchange Bybit reveals that a "sophisticated" attack led to the theft of over $1.5 billion worth of cryptocurrency from one of its Ethereum cold (offline) wallets, making it the largest ever single crypto heist in history.

author: Hacktivist groups that support Ukraine (suspected)
target: LANIT, a major Russian IT service and software provider. Specifically, its subsidiaries LLC LANTER and LLC LAN ATMservice
description: Russia's National Coordination Center for Computer Incidents (NKTsKI) discloses a hack at LANIT, a major Russian IT service provider, prompting a warning to the financial sector to change passwords and credentials.
Anne Arundel County discloses a security incident and warns residents that multiple services are still down.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Threat actors steal $49 million from Hong Kong-based stablecoin neobank Infini. The breach stemmed from compromised administrative privileges, enabling the attacker to manipulate the contract settings and siphon funds. Infini acknowledges the security breach.

author: Anubis
description: Researchers at Kela reveal that Pound Road Medical Centre (PRMC) was a target of the Anubis Ransomware-as-a-Service operation.

author: Anubis
description: Researchers at Kela reveal that Summit Home Health was a target of the Anubis Ransomware-as-a-Service operation.

Pump.fun's X account is hacked to promote a scam governance token. A fake "PUMP" token and another named "GPT-4.5" are promoted from the compromised account. The platform warns users on its Telegram channel not to interact with the hacked X account.
A cyber incident shuts down the Cleveland Municipal Court. The court remained closed for at least three days, with officials stating they had not yet confirmed the nature and scope of the event.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

author: Qilin
description: The Health Ministry of Palau experiences a ransomware attack from the Qilin ransomware group. The attack compromised patient data from the Belau National Hospital.

Researchers at QiAnXin XLab, discover a campaign that has infected Android TV devices with a botnet malware dubbed Vo1d, with the botnet scaling a peak of 1,590,299 on January 19, 2025, spanning 226 countries and regions.

target: Internet Service Providers in China and the West Coast of the U.S.
description: Researchers at Splunk discover a campaign targeting Internet service providers (ISPs) in China and the West Coast of the United States, and deploying information stealers and cryptocurrency miners on compromised hosts.
Franklin County discloses to be dealing with a ransomware attack.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

description: Missouri’s Department of Conservation is facing outages from a cyberattack.
target_class: Public admin and defence, social security

author: Qilin (a.k.a. Agenda)
description: The Qilin ransomware group attacks Covenant Health. Initially reported as a minor breach, investigations revealed that sensitive medical and personal data of nearly 480,000 individuals were compromised. The attack severely disrupted hospital operations, forcing reliance on manual paper processes for weeks.

author: Unknown Threat Actor(s)
description: Threat actors steal over $26.4 million in Ethereum from the Truebit platform. The company halts smart contract interactions while investigating with law enforcement.

author: Unknown
description: JPMorgan Chase notifies clients of a data breach at the external law firm Fried, Frank, Harris, Shriver & Jacobson LLP. The breach potentially exposed sensitive investor data, though the bank's internal systems were not compromised.

author: Unknown
description: Fintech firm Betterment confirms a data breach after attackers exploited a third-party marketing platform to send fraudulent "triple your crypto" scam emails. While the attackers accessed customer contact details—including names and birthdates—Betterment maintains that core systems, login credentials, and investment accounts remain secure and were not directly compromised.

author: Unknown
description: Betterment, a robo-advisor, discloses a data breach resulting from a social engineering attack on third-party marketing and operations platforms. While customer accounts and credentials remained secure, a threat actor accessed personal information including names and contact details, subsequently sending fraudulent crypto-related scam messages to users.

author: Unknown
description: South Korean conglomerate Kyowon Group confirms a ransomware attack that compromised 600 of its 800 servers, potentially exposing data for up to 9.6 million accounts. The group shut down systems across its education, travel, and healthcare affiliates to contain the breach while investigating the extent of the stolen information.

author: Unknown
description: Central Maine Healthcare (CMH) reports a data breach affecting 145,381 patients and employees. Between March and June 2025, unauthorized actors accessed the network, stealing sensitive personal information, including Social Security numbers and medical treatment details. CMH finalized its investigation in November 2025, offering credit monitoring to those impacted.

author: jorjortan142
target: Users of the MEXC cryptocurrency exchange
description: Researchers at Socket discover a malicious Chrome extension masquerading as a trading tool. It injects scripts into authenticated MEXC sessions to generate API keys with withdrawal rights, visually masks the permissions to deceive users, and transmits the credentials to a Telegram bot controlled by the attacker.

author: Unknown
description: The Belgian hospital AZ Monica suffers a cyberattack, forcing it to shut down all servers across its Antwerp and Deurne campuses. The incident disrupts digital medical records, leading to the cancellation of non-urgent procedures and the transfer of critical patients to other regional medical facilities.

author: Unknown
target: Over 40,000 WordPress websites using the Modular DS plugin
description: Researchers at Patchstack reveal that a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-23550, CVSS 10.0) in the WordPress "Modular DS" plugin is being actively exploited. The flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication and gain administrative access by exploiting misconfigured routing, leading to full site compromise.

author: Unknown
description: Gulshan Management Services discloses a ransomware attack.

author: SafePay
description: Ingram Micro confirms that a July 2025 ransomware attack compromised the personal data of over 42,000 individuals, including employees and job applicants. Sensitive information such as Social Security numbers and passport details was stolen by the SafePay group, which previously claimed to have exfiltrated 3.5 terabytes of internal data.

author: Unknown
target: Several Iranian state television channels tramsmitted via the Badr satellite
description: Several Iranian state television channels are briefly hijacked, interrupting regular programming to air protest footage and messages from an exiled opposition figure, according to multiple media reports.

author: US
target: Venezuelan National Power Grid (CORPOELEC) and government communications.
description: Reports link cyberattacks to the capture of Nicolás Maduro and a Venezuelan nationwide blackout. Operations targeted electrical infrastructure and military communications to facilitate "Operation Libertad." Experts suggest high-level coordination between physical tactical movements and cyber-disruption of the SCADA systems controlling the Guri Dam power plant.

author: Unnamed "malicious actor" (resembles tactics of groups like CryptoChameleon/UNC5356)
description: LastPass warns of a phishing campaign impersonating maintenance alerts to steal master passwords. Attackers send emails urging users to back up vaults within 24 hours via a malicious link. The scam creates false urgency to harvest credentials, potentially compromising all passwords stored within the victim's vault.

author: Unknown
target: Fortinet FortiGate firewall users with FortiCloud SSO enabled
description: Attackers are exploiting a patch bypass for a critical FortiGate authentication vulnerability (CVE-2025-59718). Despite being updated, admins report unauthorized logins, admin account creation, and potential configuration theft. Arctic Wolf confirms the exploitation.

author: Unknown
target: Developers and Linux systems using Python (specifically those seeking the SymPy library)
description: Security researchers at Socket discover a malicious PyPI package, sympy-dev, masquerading as a development version of the popular SymPy library. The package targets Linux systems to deploy an XMRig cryptocurrency miner using sophisticated fileless execution techniques to evade detection, while also serving as a general-purpose loader for further attacks.

author: Unknown
description: The Dresden State Art Collections (SKD) suffers a cyberattack forcing its entire IT system offline to prevent data theft. While museums remain open, internal communication and digital services are severely disrupted. Authorities are investigating the extent of the breach, with no current evidence of data exfiltration.

author: Unknown ("Hostile foreign state")
description: Russian security firm Delta confirms a major cyberattack, likely from a "hostile foreign state," which crippled its car and home alarm systems across Russia. While the company denied any data theft, the attackers claimed to have exfiltrated customer databases.

author: Unknown
description: A cyberattack on Russia’s Vladimir Bread Factory disables internal digital systems, including accounting and document management. While production remained unaffected, the disruption to order processing and delivery logistics caused regional bread shortages, forcing the manufacturer to revert to manual operations and round-the-clock shifts to maintain food supply chains.

author: Aisuru/Kimwolf botnet
target: Multiple companies, most of them in the telecommunications sector
description: The Aisuru/Kimwolf botnet launched a new massive distributed denial of service (DDoS) attack that peaked at 31.4 Tbps and 200 million requests per second, setting a new record.

author: Unknown
description: Morphisec reports a critical supply chain compromise in MicroWorld's eScan antivirus product. Malicious updates distributed via legitimate infrastructure delivered multi-stage malware that disables security features and blocks updates.

author: UAC-0001 (APT28 a.k.a. Fancy Bear and Sofacy)
description: The Ukraine’s Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT) reveals that threat actors from UAC-0001 (APT28)are exploiting CVE-2026-21509, a recently patched vulnerability in multiple versions of Microsoft Office.

author: Unknown
description: Canada Computers & Electronics says a data breach has leaked information about some of its customers.

author: Unknown
description: Step Finance reports a $40 million cryptocurrency theft resulting from the compromise of executive devices. Attackers gained access to private keys and multi-signature wallets, draining assets from the Solana-based platform. The company is currently working with law enforcement and security firms to track the stolen funds.

author: Pro-Russian threat actor called Femwar02
target: University of Rome (Università degli Studi di Roma "La Sapienza")
description: Sapienza University of Rome suffers a massive cyberattack, forcing its digital services and official website offline. The university disconnected systems to contain the breach, affecting students and staff access to administrative portals.

author: Unknown
target: Users of Metro Systems Metro4 software
description: Researchers at VulnCheck report that CVE-2025-11953 (a.k.a Metro4Shell), a critical command injection vulnerability in Metro Systems Metro4 software, is being exploited in the wild. Attackers can execute arbitrary code on vulnerable servers, compromising warehouse management and retail operations. Organizations are urged to patch immediately to prevent full system takeover.
country: Global (specifically regions where Metro AG operates, such as DE, RO, PL)

author: ‘GordonFreeman’
target: Spain’s Ministry of Science, Innovation, and Universities (Ministerio de Ciencia, Innovación y Universidades)
description: Spain's Ministry of Science (Ministerio de Ciencia) announces a partial shutdown of its IT systems, affecting several citizen- and company-facing services. A threat actor with the moniker ‘GordonFreeman’ claims an attack on the institution's systems and publishes data samples as proof of the breach.

author: Warlock
description: SmarterTools confirms that the Warlock ransomware gang breached its network after compromising an email system, but it did not impact business applications or account data.

author: Unknown
description: Researchers at Sysdig disclose the details of an AI-assisted cloud intrusion that escalated from initial access to full administrator privileges in just eight minutes, leveraging LLMs to analyze misconfigurations and automate exploitation.

author: Qilin (a.k.a. Agenda)
description: Romanian oil pipeline operator Conpet disclosed a cyberattack claimed by the Qilin ransomware gang. The company shut down some systems to contain the breach, though transport operations allegedly remain unaffected. Qilin claimed the theft of over 30GB of data, including financial records and employee information, threatening its release.

author: Infy (a.k.a. Prince of Persia)
target: Undisclosed Organizations in India and Germany
description: Unnamed threat actor (Campaign tracked as ClawHavoc)

author: U.S.
target: Separate military system connected to the nuclear sites at Fordow, Natanz and Isfahan
description: According to several U.S. officials, the U.S. military disrupted Iranian air missile defense systems as part of a coordinated operation to destroy the country’s nuclear program, during 2025 strikes

author: NoName057(16)
target: Italian diplomatic missions abroad and sites linked to the upcoming Winter Olympics
description: Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani reveals that Italy thwarted a series of cyberattacks of “Russian origin” targeting diplomatic missions abroad and sites linked to the upcoming Winter Olympics. The attempted attacks hit multiple foreign ministry offices “starting with Washington,” according to Tajani, as well as facilities connected to the 2026 Winter Games, including hotels in the Alpine resort of Cortina d'Ampezzo. The pro-Russian hacker group NoName057(16) claimed responsibility, describing the campaign as retaliation for Italy’s support for Ukraine.

author: ShinyHunters (a.k.a. UNC6040, SLSH, Scattered LAPSUS$ Hunters)
description: The ShinyHunters threat actor claims responsibility for last year’s data breaches at the Harvard University and publishes what it claims are more than 1 million records from the university on the group’s dedicated leak site, which the gang uses to extort its victims.

author: Unknown
target: Organizations using Zendesk and their IT/Security administrators
description: A massive spam wave is hitting Zendesk users, flooding them with thousands of "activate your account" emails. Attackers exploit Zendesk's signup process to bypass spam filters, aiming to bury important security alerts or overwhelm IT support systems.

author: Unknown
description: The European Commission investigates a breach after finding evidence that its mobile device management platform was hacked, allegedly exploiting the two Ivanti critical code injection vulnerabilities tracked as CVE-2026-1281 and CVE-2026-1340

author: Qilin
description: ApolloMD discloses that the ransomware attack claimed by the Qilin ransomware group compromised the information of 620,000 individuals.

author: Unknown
description: CVE-2026-1731, a critical pre-authentication remote code execution vulnerability in BeyondTrust Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access appliances is now being exploited in attacks after a PoC was published online.

author: Unknown
description: The Washington Hotel brand in Japan announces that that its servers were compromised in a ransomware attack, exposing various business data.

author: Unknown
description: A large-scale DDoS attack targets Deutsche Bahn, causing significant disruptions to its digital services and passenger information systems.

author: Unknown
description: A poorly crafted phishing campaign uses a fake PDF security incident report hosted on AWS to scare victims into enabling 2FA.

author: Rhysida
target: Government of the Cheyenne and Arapaho Tribes
description: The government of the Cheyenne and Arapaho Tribes is being extorted by the Rhysida gang, after a ransomware attack shut down its schools and critical systems in January.

author: State-sponsored actors using Predator spyware
target: Teixeira Cândido – an Angolan journalist, jurist, press freedom activist, and former Secretary-General of the Syndicate of Angolan Journalists (Sindicato dos Journalists Angolanos).
description: Researchers at Amnesty International uncover a targeted campaign using Predator spyware against an Angolan journalist.

author: Unknown
description: The University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) closes all its clinic locations statewide on Thursday following a ransomware attack.

author: Unknown
description: Advantest Corporation discloses that its corporate network has been targeted in a ransomware attack that may have affected customer or employee data.

author: Unknown
description: In a software supply chain attack, the open-source, AI-powered coding assistant Cline CLI is updated to stealthily install OpenClaw, a self-hosted autonomous AI agent.

author: MuddyWater (a.k.a. TEMP.Zagros, Seedworm Static Kitten, TA450, Boggy Serpens, Earth Vetala)
target: Organizations and individuals primarily across the MENA region
description: Researchers at Group-IB detail "Operation Olalampo," a campaign by MuddyWater, resulting in the deployment of new malware families that share overlapping samples previously identified as used by the threat actor. These include downloaders like GhostFetch and HTTP_VIP, along with a Rust backdoor called CHAR and an advanced implant codenamed GhostBackDoor that's dropped by GhostFetch.

author: Unknown
description: Ukraine’s central bank says its online store for collectible coins and numismatic products was temporarily taken offline after a cyberattack exposed some customer information.

author: INC
description: Air Côte d'Ivoire, the main airline serving the West African nation of Côte d'Ivoire is hit with a cyberattack that forced it to institute business continuity plans.

author: Unknown
description: The United Arab Emirates say it stopped a ransomware attack that allegedly targeted the country’s digital infrastructure.

author: Russia-aligned Mercenary Akula (tracked by CERT UA as UAC-0050)
description: Researchers at BlueVoyant observer the Russia-aligned threat actor Akula targeting a European financial institution as part of a social engineering attack to likely facilitate intelligence gathering or financial theft.

author: Unknown
description: South Korea’s National Tax Service exposes publicly the mnemonic recovery phrase of a seized cryptocurrency wallet and someone is able to steal $4.4 million in crypto assets.,

author: Unknown
target: Websites operated by Roskomnadzor (Russian communications watchdog) and the Russian Defense Ministry, as well as infrastructure belonging to the Main Radio Frequency Center (GRFC)
description: Russia’s internet regulator (Roskomnadzor) and defense ministry say their servers were hit by a large distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack that briefly disrupted access to several government websites.

author: Unknown
description: A Chrome extension named "QuickLens - Search Screen with Google Lens" is removed from the Chrome Web Store after it was compromised to push malware and attempt to steal crypto from thousands of users.

author: US and Israel
target: Critical infrastructure, official news sites, and security communications systems in Iran
description: Cyberattacks conducted by US-Israeli forces caused widespread disruptions in Iran, including to news/propaganda websites (such as IRNA news agency), communications infrastructure used by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), local applications, and digital government services.

author: Unknown
description: Threat actors are exploiting CVE-2026-1492, a critical vulnerability in the User Registration & Membership plugin, which is installed on more than 60,000 WordPress sites.

author: Unknown
description: Cloud Imperium Games (CIG), the game company behind Star Citizen and Squadron 42, says attackers breached systems containing some users' personal information in January.

author: FulcrumSec
description: American data analytics company LexisNexis Legal & Professional confirms that threat actors breached its servers and accessed some customer and business information, after a threat actor named FulcrumSec leaks 2GB of files.

author: Unknown
description: Password management software provider LastPass warns users of a phishing campaign targeting its users with fake unauthorized account access alerts.

author: Unknown
description: Passaic County, one of the largest counties in New Jersey reveals to be dealing with a cyberattack that disrupted the phone lines and IT systems used across government offices.

author: Suspected Iranian threat actors
target: Internet-connected surveillance cameras (specifically Hikvision and Dahua brands) in israel, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, the UAE, Cyprus and Lebanon
description: Researchers at Check Point reveal a surge in attempts to compromise internet-connected surveillance cameras across the Middle East during the ongoing regional conflict, with activity attributed to infrastructure linked to Iranian threat actors.
country: IL QA BH KW AE LB

author: Ololoshka562
description: The Wikimedia Foundation suffers a security incident today after a self-propagating JavaScript worm began modifying user scripts and vandalizing Meta-Wiki pages.

author: Unknown
description: TriZetto Provider Solutions, a healthcare IT company that develops software and services used by health insurers and healthcare providers discloses a data breach that exposed the sensitive information of over 3.4 million people.

author: Unknown
description: Ericsson Inc., the U.S. subsidiary of Swedish networking and telecommunications giant Ericsson, says threat actors have stolen data belonging to over 15,000 employees and customers after hacking one of its service providers.

author: Camaro Dragon (Chinese-nexus threat actor)
description: Researchers at Check Point reveal the Chinese-nexus threat actor Camaro Dragon attempted to deploy a variant of PlugX malware against Qatari targets within one day of the launch of Operation Epic Fury and the onset of the escalation in the Middle East

author: Chinese-nexus threat actor
description: Check Point Research observe another attack targeting Qatar and using a password-protected archive, likely delivered via email. The campaign employed low-quality AI-generated lures impersonating the Israeli government to deliver a previously unseen Rust-based loader.

author: Unknown
description: Starbucks discloses a data breach affecting hundreds of employees after threat actors gained access to their Starbucks Partner Central accounts, compromising 889 Starbucks Partner Central accounts used to manage employment details, personal information, benefits, and HR information.

author: Homeland Justice
description: Albania’s parliament says that it had been targeted by a “sophisticated” cyberattack aimed at deleting data and compromising several internal systems.

author: Unknown
target: Users and repositories utilizing the xygeni/xygeni-action@v5 GitHub Action (estimated at over 130 repositories).
description: Xygeni discovers a supply chain attack where compromised credentials were used to "poison" the v5 tag of their official GitHub Action. The attacker injected a command-and-control (C2) reverse shell, enabling arbitrary command execution.

author: Handala
description: Leading medical technology company Stryker is hit by a wiper malware attack claimed by Handala, an Iranian-linked and pro-Palestinian hacktivist group.

author: Unknown
target: Child Exploitation Forensic Lab in the FBI’s New York Field Office
description: An unidentified foreign hacker broke into the FBI’s field office in New York in 2023 and compromised files related to the bureau’s investigation into the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

author: GlassWorm
target: Hundreds of GitHub Python repositories and their maintainers/users (including Django apps, ML research, and PyPI packages)
description: Researchers at StepSecurity discover "ForceMemo," a sophisticated campaign where attackers use compromised credentials from malicious VS Code extensions to force-push malware into hundreds of GitHub Python repositories. The malware uses the Solana blockchain for command-and-control instructions and downloads Node.js to execute encrypted payloads, specifically avoiding Russian systems.

author: Ukrainian Cyber Alliance
description: The Russian city of Perm restores its parking payment system after a cyberattack last week knocked the service offline and temporarily made parking free for several days.

author: Threat actors using the Kratos Phishing-as-a-Service Kit
target: C-level executive at security firm Outpost24
description: Researchers at SpecOps reveal the details of a sophisticated multi-chain redirect phishing campaign exploiting legitimate Cisco and Nylas domains to target a C-level executive at its parent company Outpost24

author: Unknown
target: Nordstrom customers and the company's email/communication infrastructure
description: Attackers abuse Nordstrom's official email system to send cryptocurrency scams to customers. Disguised as a St. Patrick's Day promotion," the emails promised a 200% return on deposits. The breach likely involved compromised Okta and Salesforce credentials, allowing legitimate infrastructure to distribute fraudulent messages.

author: APT28 (a.ka. Fancy Bear, Sednit, Strontium, Pawn Storm, Sofacy)
target: Ukrainian State Hydrology Agency (under the Ministry of Infrastructure)
description: Seqrite Labs discovers "Operation GhostMail," a Russian-linked APT campaign exploiting the Zimbra Webmail XSS vulnerability (CVE-2025-66376) to compromise a Ukrainian government agency. The attack uses obfuscated JavaScript embedded in a phishing email to exfiltrate credentials, 2FA codes, and 90 days of mailbox content via DNS and HTTPS.

author: Lazarus Group (a.k.a. Guardians of Peace, or Hidden Cobra).
description: Bitrefill attributes a recent infrastructure breach to North Korea’s Lazarus Group. After compromising an employee’s laptop, attackers drained hot wallets, exploited gift card inventory, and stole 18,500 purchase records. The company identified the state-sponsored group through malware analysis, on-chain tracing, and reused IP addresses.

author: Unknown
description: The educational services company Kaplan notifies state regulators that 1.4 million people had Social Security and driver’s license numbers leaked following a cybersecurity incident in the fall of 2025.

author: Unknown
description: Navia Benefit Solutions, Inc. (Navia) informs nearly 2.7 million individuals of a data breach that exposed their sensitive information to attackers.

author: Unknown
target: Approximately 100 organizations, in the manufacturing, retail, and healthcare primarily in the United States
description: Researchers at Microsoft discover tax-themed phishing emails containing an attachment redirecting to a phishing page built with the SneakyLog PhaaS platform and mimicking the Microsoft 365 sign-in page to steal credentials.

author: Unknown
target: Multiple accounting companies and few related industries such as financial services, legal, and insurance in the U.S.
description: Researchers at Microsoft discover a campaign targeting CPAs, asking for assistance in filing taxes, asking for a quote, and typically providing a backstory, and distributing the remote management and monitoring (RMM) tool Datto.

author: Unknown
target: Multiple organizations in the U.S. including financial services, technology and software, and retail and consumer goods
description: Researchers at Microsoft observe a large-scale phishing campaign sent to more than 29,000 users across 10,000 organizations, impersonating the the IRS, and distributing the remote management and monitoring (RMM) tool ScreenConnect.

author: WorldPay
description: Los Angeles Metro says it's working to restore access to its internal administrative computers after the agency's security team discovered "unauthorized activity." The WorldPay ransomware group claims responsibility for the attack.

author: TeamPCP
target: Users of the Aqua Security Trivy scanner
description: The Trivy vulnerability scanner is compromised in a supply-chain attack by threat actors known as TeamPCP, which distributed credential-stealing malware through official releases and GitHub Actions.

author: Unknown
description: Foster City, California is forced to pause all public services outside of emergency responses in light of a ransomware attack.

author: Unknown
description: Trio-Tech International, a semiconductor testing company warns regulators that its subsidiary in Singapore suffered a ransomware attack.

author: Unknown
description: A cyberattack on vehicle breathalyzer company Intoxalock disables backend calibration systems, stranding thousands of US drivers with court-mandated ignition interlocks. The disruption prevents mandatory maintenance, locking vehicles and highlighting the risks of centralized, internet-connected automotive safety infrastructure.

author: Unknown
description: Heritage Bank discloses that threat actors copied files from an internal employee file-sharing server. The stolen data includes employees’ and customers’ private and confidential information, such as names, Social Security numbers, account numbers, dates of birth, and addresses.

author: TeamPCP
target: Users of the Aqua Security Trivy scanner
description: Researchers at Aikido Security identify a destructive TeamPCP campaign using "CanisterWorm" malware to target Kubernetes clusters. The malware fingerprints systems; those identified as Iranian via timezone or locale are wiped using a "kamikaze" DaemonSet, while others receive a persistent backdoor disguised as PostgreSQL monitoring tools.

author: TeamPCP
target: Aqua Security (specifically their Trivy container scanning tool and GitHub organization).
description: Researchers at Socket reveal that compromised Trivy Docker images (0.69.4–0.69.6) were pushed to Docker Hub, bypassing GitHub releases. These images contained "TeamPCP" infostealer malware, utilizing a typosquatted C2 domain for exfiltration. The breach targeted Aqua Security’s supply chain, potentially affecting numerous downstream CI/CD pipelines and third-party integrations using automated updates.

author: Unknown
description: Decentralized finance platform Resolv reveals that a cyberattack allowed a threat actor to compromise the company’s infrastructure and illicitly create $80 million worth of its USR stablecoin.

author: Unknown
description: Popular anime streaming platform Crunchyroll is investigating a breach after hackers claimed to have stolen personal information for approximately 6.8 million people.

author: Unknown
description: The Dutch Ministry of Finance confirms that some of its systems were breached in a cyberattack detected last week.

author: Unknown
description: Bug bounty platform HackerOne notifies hundreds of employees that their data was stolen after attackers hacked Navia, one of its U.S. benefits administrators.

author: TeamPCP
description: Researchers at Sysdig discover that TeamPCP expanded its supply chain attack from Aqua Security’s Trivy to Checkmarx’s GitHub Actions. By harvesting credentials from CI/CD runner memory, the actor poisoned multiple trusted actions, exfiltrating encrypted secrets to vendor-specific typosquatted domains.

author: Unknown
description: Hightower Holding, the parent company of financial management services provider Hightower Advisors, notifies over 130,000 individuals of a data breach.

author: ShinyHunters
description: Infinite Campus, a widely used K-12 student information system, warns customers of a data breach following an extortion attempt by the threat actor ShinyHunters. The threat actor compromised an employee’s Salesforce account via a sophisticated "vishing" campaign, exfiltrating staff contact details and directory information. The company refused ransom demands.

author: TeamPCP
description: Researchers at Endor Labs discover a supply chain attack by "TeamPCP" targeting the litellm Python library. Attackers published backdoored versions (1.82.7, 1.82.8) to PyPI containing a credential harvester, Kubernetes lateral movement toolkit, and a persistent systemd backdoor, targeting developers and cloud environments to exfiltrate secrets and SSH keys.

author: Unknown
target: An unnamed multinational car manufacturer, along with other e-commerce stores using Adobe Commerce/Magento.
description: Researchers at Sansec discover a payment skimmer targeting the ‘PolyShell’ vulnerability in version 2 of Magento Open Source and Adobe Commerce installations. It bypasses Content Security Policy (CSP) and traditional HTTP-based defenses by using WebRTC DataChannel, to load its malicious payload and exfiltrate stolen credit card data directly to the attacker's server.

author: Unknown
description: Healthcare management services provider QualDerm Partners notifies more than 3.1 million people that their personal, medical, and health insurance information was stolen in a December 2025 data breach.

author: Unknown
description: Puerto Rico’s Department of Transportation is forced to cancel all upcoming appointments at the agency that handles driver’s licenses, permits and vehicle registrations due to a cyberattack.

author: Unknown
description: A ransomware attack disrupts digital systems at Spain’s Port of Vigo, forcing authorities to disconnect parts of its network and temporarily manage cargo operations manually.

author: Unknown
target: Marketing teams and business users managing TikTok for Business and Google Ad Manager accounts
description: Researchers at Push Security discover a sophisticated phishing campaign targeting "TikTok for Business" and Google accounts. Attackers use "Adversary-in-the-Middle" (AITM) kits to steal session tokens, bypassing MFA. These compromised accounts are typically exploited for malvertising scams, ad fraud, and distributing further malware via trusted business profiles.

author: TA446 (a.k.a. Star Blizzard, Callisto, ColdRiver, SeaBorgium)
description: Researchers at Proofpoint reveal that the Russian state-sponsored group tracked as Star Blizzard has adopted the DarkSword iOS exploit kit in an ongoing campaign.

author: ShinyHunters
description: The European Commission is investigating a data breach after a threat actor compromised an Amazon Web Services account. The threat actor claimed to exfiltrate over 350 GB of data, including employee information and databases. Few days later the European Commission confirms the breach claimed by the ShinyHunters group.

author: TeamPCP
target: Developers and organizations using the Telnyx Python SDK
description: Attackers from TeamPCP hijack the popular Telnyx Python SDK on PyPI to distribute credential-stealing malware. The attack utilized steganography to hide malicious code within WAV audio files. The malware targets SSH keys, cloud tokens, and Kubernetes secrets across Linux, macOS, and Windows systems.

author: Unknown
description: Security researchers from multiple company observe massive reconnaissance activity for CVE-2026-3055, a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway. Few days later the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) orders government agencies to patch their Citrix NetScaler appliances.

author: Qilin (a.k.a. Agenda)
description: The German political party Die Linke suffers a severe cyberattack targeting its IT infrastructure. Upon discovery, the party isolates systems to prevent data theft. The incident was later linked to the Qilin ransomware group.

author: Unknown
description: Healthcare IT firm CareCloud discloses a data breach incident that exposed sensitive data and caused a network disruption lasting approximately eight hours.

author: UAC-0255
target: State organizations, medical centers, security companies, educational institutions, financial institutions, and software development companies in Ukraine
description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) discloses details of a new phishing campaign in which the cybersecurity agency itself was impersonated to distribute a remote administration tool known as AGEWHEEZE.

author: UNC1069
target: The Axios npm package (and by extension, the millions of developers and automated CI/CD systems that download it)
description: Researchers at StepSecurity reveal that attackers compromised the Axios npm package by hijacking the lead maintainer's account to publish malicious versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4. Few days later Google formally attributes the supply chain compromise to a financially motivated North Korean threat activity cluster tracked as UNC1069.

author: Unknown
description: Researchers at Proofpoint observe a campaign impersonating the U.S. IRS. The lure purported to relate to the target’s recent IRS filing.

author: Unknown
description: Researchers at Proofpoint reveal the details of a campaign impersonating company executives, human resources, or vendor/supplier contacts in attempts to steal financial and personal data, likely with a goal of leveraging it for follow-on fraud.

author: Unknown
description: Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital (NMH) notifies 250,000 people that their personal and health information was compromised in a data breach.
